_comment: Benchmark tasks for squad-bench. `cwd: "sandbox"` runs against a fresh temp copy of ./sandbox (git-seeded, isolated). `expect` is a substring, or /regex/ (case-insensitive), matched against the final answer; omit it for open-ended tasks. Add your own tasks freely — same shape.

tasks.prompt: Use your code_scout teammate to find where retry-with-backoff logic is implemented in this codebase. Report the function name and file:line. Read-only — let Scout do the searching, do not read files yourself.
tasks.notes: Single-target search. Weak scout should find it in ~1 grep; measures over-search (subagent_tools grep count) and redispatches.

tasks.prompt: I need two independent things located in this codebase. Delegate the searching to your code_scout teammate (you may fan out). (1) Where is retry-with-backoff implemented? (2) Where is the LRU cache type defined? Report each with the symbol name and file:line. Read-only.
tasks.notes: Exercises parallel fan-out (code_scout max_instances). Both targets exist in the sandbox.

tasks.prompt: Have code_scout report the exact fields of the LruCache struct in this codebase (name and type of each). Read-only.
tasks.notes: Precise symbol read (lsp_read_symbol / small Read). 'order' is the last field, so mentioning it proves the whole struct was read.

tasks.prompt: Ask your code_docs teammate: in the Go standard library, what does strings.Cut return and what is its exact signature? Report the signature and the meaning of each return value.
tasks.notes: Network task (code_docs uses web search). Skip in offline runs. strings.Cut returns (before, after string, found bool).
